title: Visualization of a Graph and its Incidence Matrix
description: A side-by-side comparison of a simple undirected graph and its corresponding incidence matrix, showing how edges connect vertices and how that relationship is represented numerically.
elements: Graph with labeled vertices (V1, V2, V3, V4)
Edges connecting the vertices (e1, e2, e3, e4, e5)
Incidence Matrix table with rows for vertices and columns for edges
relationships: The graph's structure defines the values in the matrix
A '1' in a matrix cell indicates the vertex (row) is incident to the edge (column)
The matrix is a tabular representation of the vertex-edge incidence relationships in the graph
